purpose: Deterministic UI/UX contract for reducing cognitive load, accelerating orientation, preserving diagnostic reasoning flow, strengthening memory encoding, improving error recovery, sustaining long study sessions, and reinforcing trust across P@thfndr didactics.
governingPrinciple: The interface should disappear psychologically so the learner feels oriented, guided, immersed, rewarded, and intellectually engaged rather than burdened by software.

requiredRegions.purpose: Tell the user where they are, what the topic is, why it matters, and what to do next.
Support the active task: tutorial reading, image review, MCQ solving, or case interpretation.
Hold pearls, pitfalls, context links, differential anchors, and optional deep dives.
Surface progress updates, mastery changes, discrepancy alerts, remediation links, and next actions.

recommendedLayout.bottomDock: notes, review queue, and follow-up tasks

rules: The user must be able to identify where they are, what matters most, and what to do next within one viewport.
Typography, spacing, grouping, contrast, and motion must establish an obvious next step.
Equal visual weight across unrelated elements is forbidden.
failConditions: Dense undifferentiated text blocks above the fold.
Multiple competing primary actions in the same visual tier.

rules: Core navigation locations must remain stable across the platform.
Navigation must use icon and text together.
Current location must always be visibly highlighted.

breadcrumbs.example: CP > Transfusion Medicine > Hemolytic Reactions > DAT Interpretation

navigationFramework.destinationViewRule: Selecting a tutorial, lecture, case, or teaching session must open a destination view with a clear route back to the library context.

progressiveDisclosure.rule: Do not present all details at once. Show overview first, then reveal details, pearls, pitfalls, and deep dives incrementally.

whitespace.rule: Whitespace must be treated as an instructional aid that reduces fatigue and improves comprehension.

immediateContextFrame.placement: top of every tutorial or teaching-session destination page

feedbackLoops.rule: Every meaningful user action must produce visible feedback.

rules: Decorative fonts are forbidden in instructional surfaces.

iconography.rule: Use one unified icon system. Icons must reinforce meaning and reduce reading burden rather than decorate.

predictability.rule: Buttons, cards, previews, keyboard shortcuts, and navigation outcomes must behave consistently across the platform.

entryState.rule: The didactics landing state must answer 'What should I do right now?' immediately.

routeAcceptanceGates: Every didactics destination route must show an orientation frame before the main instructional body.
Every selected tutorial or teaching session must open as a destination view rather than remaining only a list-card highlight.
Every route must preserve state on resume unless a user intentionally resets context.
Every wrong answer must expose explanation plus remediation routing.
Every core didactics page must make the AP versus CP scope obvious within one viewport.
Every interactive surface must preserve stable control placement and predictable navigation outcomes.
uiUxAuditChecklist: Can a new user identify current location within 3 seconds?
Can the user infer the next best action without scrolling?
Does the page expose scope, difficulty, time, and board relevance immediately?
Does the route preserve prior state after leaving and returning?
Does an incorrect answer trigger explanation and next-step remediation?
Is CP content surfaced with equal clarity to AP content where applicable?
Do colors, icons, and motion retain one meaning each across the surface?
